objective: Extend the run2 deck visual vocabulary beyond stacked rectangles by assigning each page role a content-serving, non-card primary visual grammar.

artifact_scope.quality_gate: Every page role must name a non-rectangular or non-card primary structure, and the structure must carry the page proof object rather than act as decoration.

source_inputs.use_in_this_artifact: Source layout archetype, public first-read object, composition lock, and native module intent.
Source semantic objects and observable scene evidence that each grammar module must make visible.
Source object hierarchy, forbidden card/grid fallbacks, and composition quality checks.

must_do: draw the primary structure before labels, proof chips, badges, or captions
make scale, crop, overlap, perspective, masking, curved paths, or field topology carry hierarchy
bind each page's proof object to the primary structure so the structure explains the content
keep trace ids, memory ids, gate ids, and raw source terms off the public slide surface
use native PPT vectors, masks, gradients, type, connectors, and editable grouped objects
must_not_do: use three equal feature cards as the primary structure
use a generic dashboard grid as the primary structure
put a process diagram inside a large rounded card
let a bottom legend carry the main information
copy source media, source screenshots, or source brand layouts
minimum_non_card_signals_per_page: one dominant composed object or field
one secondary depth layer that is visibly subordinate
one connective structure such as a route, lens, contour, orbit, lane, gate, or path
one negative-space text socket attached to the structure rather than boxed away from it

main_structure.name: deck_object_emerging_from_source_memory_field
non_rectangular_or_non_card_basis: oversized deck surface is cropped beyond the frame and tilted in perspective
source memory is a layered field of contours and orbiting controls rather than equal tiles
supporting proof marks attach to the deck edge and field routes instead of sitting in cards
main_structure.serves_content_by: The viewer reads the real brief, source memory, editable deck, and review boundary as one product path before seeing any explanatory label.

draw_order: draw a shallow diagonal memory field across the background with contour bands and curved route lines
place one oversized editable launch deck object on the front plane, cropped at one or two edges
attach brief capsule, source-memory control, and quiet review-boundary marker to the deck perimeter
lock the headline to a negative-space pocket created by the deck crop, not inside a box
add small proof labels only after the product object reads at contact-sheet scale
forbidden_fallbacks: decorative hero with no product object
large report panel explaining the workflow
page_type_to_visual_grammar.success_probe: At thumbnail scale, the cover must read as one generated launch deck emerging from a source-memory field, not as a stack of labeled blocks.

main_structure.name: source_memory_compiler_field
non_rectangular_or_non_card_basis: source observations enter as an irregular source wall, not a uniform file grid
the compiler is drawn as a compressed field bend and route turn, not a node box
the proof object sits at the field's destination edge with path tension from the source side
main_structure.serves_content_by: The field shows why drawing waits until source observations become memory, obligations, and proof objects.

draw_order: draw a wide oblique field band that bends once from source side to proof side
scatter source fragments along the entry edge with varied scale and partial masking
compress fragments through a central memory condenser drawn as nested contours and route pressure
surface the message obligation and proof object at the destination as one stable composed object
place copy along the field's open edge so labels follow the transformation path
page_type_to_visual_grammar.success_probe: A reviewer should be able to point to the field bend and explain source-to-memory transformation without reading a process list.

main_structure.name: small_before_thumbnail_vs_dominant_after_stage
non_rectangular_or_non_card_basis: before state is a recessed, partly clipped thumbnail on a dim side stage
after state is a large angled product surface occupying the front stage
the delta is a lens or spotlight bridging the depth gap rather than a center column
main_structure.serves_content_by: The theater makes the product difference a mechanism gap: prompt-only collapses, while Vulca continues through source memory, proof routing, design moves, and editable output.

draw_order: draw a darkened side-stage pocket for the small before surface
draw the after surface large and forward with perspective, crop, and brighter state
insert a route break between them using a jagged path, dimmed connector, or interruption notch
attach the delta lens to the after object and name the four gained mechanisms there
keep the headline compressed near the after stage so the after state carries the argument
forbidden_fallbacks: two equal before and after cards
page_type_to_visual_grammar.success_probe: The after surface must dominate the before thumbnail, and the viewer must understand that the difference is mechanism rather than taste.

main_structure.name: active_decision_lane_on_inspection_workbench
non_rectangular_or_non_card_basis: the workspace is a single angled workbench plane, not separate UI cards
source, decision, output, and gate are connected by one active lane with depth layers
proof markers are embedded as pins, tabs, and overlays on the workbench surface
main_structure.serves_content_by: The active lane lets viewers inspect capability binding, contract match, content check, and bad-control failure without turning proof into a table.

draw_order: draw one angled workbench plane spanning the page with a visible active lane
place source, decision, output, and release gate along the lane as attached surfaces with unequal scale
make the decision pane the brightest and most legible object because it proves mapping, not storage
pin compact proof markers to exact lane points instead of listing them in a side table
reserve a small off-canvas route affordance for raw records and keep the public canvas readable
page_type_to_visual_grammar.success_probe: A reviewer should see an inspectable product workspace with one active proof lane, not a compliance dashboard.

main_structure.name: single_editable_ppt_preview_hero_object
non_rectangular_or_non_card_basis: one generated result object owns most of the frame and is cropped like a cinematic product shot
before, memory, code, and release states fuse into the object's surface and perimeter
proof rail orbits behind or below the object rather than splitting into cards
main_structure.serves_content_by: The climax proves convergence: brief, source memory, mapped design moves, renderer output, editable preview, and QA proof become one result object.

draw_order: draw the editable PPT preview first at the largest scale in the deck
use perspective, partial crop, and one bright edge to create a product-reveal moment
embed memory route, code strip, editable state, and QA proof as fused surface details
place the proof rail behind or under the hero object with lower contrast and smaller scale
delay labels until the object is visually self-explanatory
forbidden_fallbacks: internal QA criteria as public labels
page_type_to_visual_grammar.success_probe: The slide must feel like one editable product result being revealed, not a recap of disconnected workflow pieces.

main_structure.name: forked_release_handoff_gate
non_rectangular_or_non_card_basis: the decision surface is a directional map with a fork and gate, not a checklist grid
generated proof, human review, public block, and next run are placed on a single route
the blocked state is a calm barrier in the path rather than a red status card
main_structure.serves_content_by: The map protects credibility by showing that the next proof is generated and public release waits for visual review, native inspection, source-boundary review, and human approval.

draw_order: draw one route from story-ready input to generated-proof output
insert a visible human-review gate before public release using a path barrier, hinge, or decision fork
place release-blocked state on the public branch as a restrained wall, not a celebratory badge
place next-run action on the continuing branch with stronger direction than decoration
attach final copy to the route's destination side and keep full checklist detail off-canvas
page_type_to_visual_grammar.success_probe: The close must resolve as a calm handoff map with one dominant next action and one visible release gate.

use_when: a page needs to show transformation, pressure, or selection before a final object exists
the content is a source-to-memory, evidence-to-obligation, or route-forming mechanism
equal cards would hide the causal path
visual_grammar_modules.primary_structure: One oblique field or landscape that bends from input pressure to output obligation.
how_to_draw: start with a large freeform field band that crosses the canvas diagonally or in a shallow arc
use contours, nested translucent masks, varied fragment scale, and partial crops to avoid a rectangular panel read
place semantic objects along the field according to causal order: source fragments, memory condenser, obligation, proof object
make the destination object stable and calmer than the entry fragments
place text in negative-space sockets created by the field edge, never inside the field as a caption box
visual_grammar_modules.content_service: The field itself explains how raw source observations become product memory and proof obligations.

use_when: a page must make a contrast legible through scale and consequence
the after state is the argument and the before state is intentionally underpowered
the comparison is about mechanism, not aesthetic preference
visual_grammar_modules.primary_structure: An asymmetric stage where a recessed before thumbnail is overpowered by a large forward after surface.
how_to_draw: draw the before object small, dim, and pushed into a side-stage pocket
draw the after object large, cropped, and closer to the viewer with a brighter product surface
break or dim the route from before to after at the missing mechanism
attach the delta lens, proof marker, or consequence strip to the after surface
compress copy around the after object so the after state owns the claim
visual_grammar_modules.content_service: The theater makes the missing mechanism visible without relying on a matrix or verbal scoring.

use_when: a page needs to prove inspectability without exposing raw records
multiple evidence states must stay connected to one product action
table-like proof would overwhelm the public slide
visual_grammar_modules.primary_structure: A single angled workbench plane with one active decision lane and embedded proof pins.
how_to_draw: draw one continuous workspace surface before any pane, row, or label
run one active lane through the workspace and place proof states on the lane
vary scale and z-order so source, decision, output, and gate are related but not equal
embed proof markers as pins, tabs, overlays, or small surface annotations
send raw evidence to a small viewer route marker instead of placing it on the main surface
visual_grammar_modules.content_service: The workspace lets the viewer inspect source, decision, output, and gate as one product state.

use_when: a page needs one product object to own the first read
the business claim is best proven by seeing the generated deck or editable result
supporting systems should appear as depth, perimeter, or fused surface details
visual_grammar_modules.primary_structure: One dominant generated product surface that emerges from, or absorbs, its supporting system.
how_to_draw: draw the product object first at poster or hero scale
crop it beyond the canvas or tilt it slightly so it cannot read as a flat card
fuse source memory, code, review, and proof signals into the object's surface or perimeter
keep support rails behind, below, or orbiting the object with clearly lower visual priority
add labels only after the product surface is legible without them
visual_grammar_modules.content_service: The reveal turns the abstract workflow into a concrete generated, editable artifact.
avoid: separate system cards around a small output

use_when: a page needs to show status, gate, and next action without claiming release
the important structure is a decision path or handoff boundary
a checklist would imply completion or flatten priority
visual_grammar_modules.primary_structure: A directional route with a visible gate, fork, blocked branch, and next-run destination.
how_to_draw: draw the route first as one continuous path with a clear direction
place generated proof on the path before the human-review gate
show public release as a branch held behind a quiet blocked gate
make the next-run branch the readable destination without celebratory styling
keep detailed release criteria off-canvas or in speaker notes
visual_grammar_modules.content_service: The map explains what is ready, what is blocked, and what must happen next in one glance.

module_geometry_blueprints.primary_structure_is: a bent freeform field whose contour and pressure show transformation

native_ppt_shape_plan.why_not_card: curved edge, diagonal route, and changing thickness prevent a panel read

native_ppt_shape_plan.path_hint: three concentric open contours around x=48 y=47, compressed along the route bend
native_ppt_shape_plan.semantic_role: source fragments become memory and obligations

native_ppt_shape_plan.bounds_hint: x=67 y=34 w=24 h=23, tilted -4deg, clipped by field edge
native_ppt_shape_plan.semantic_role: stable proof object at the end of transformation

content_attachment_points: source fragments attach along x=6..28 y=58..76
memory labels attach outside the bend at x=42..54 y=33..42
proof object labels attach to destination perimeter at x=72..91 y=29..58

module_geometry_blueprints.primary_structure_is: an asymmetric stage with depth, route break, and after-state dominance

native_ppt_shape_plan.bounds_hint: x=7 y=43 w=22 h=25, tilted 5deg, clipped by a dark side-stage mask
native_ppt_shape_plan.why_not_card: small scale, partial clipping, and dim side-stage treatment make it subordinate

native_ppt_shape_plan.path_hint: connector C 27 55, 36 48, 42 45 is interrupted at x=34..39; lens sits on after edge x=40..55 y=38..55

content_attachment_points: before label stays inside side-stage pocket only as a low-weight state cue
mechanism labels attach to the route_break_lens
main claim anchors to the after_front_stage negative space

module_geometry_blueprints.primary_structure_is_not: a dashboard grid or compliance table
module_geometry_blueprints.primary_structure_is: one angled workbench plane with an active decision lane embedded in it

native_ppt_shape_plan.why_not_card: the proof states live on one skewed plane rather than separate equal containers

native_ppt_shape_plan.placement_hint: four pins on active_decision_lane at t=.18,.43,.68,.89
native_ppt_shape_plan.semantic_role: capability bound, contract matched, content checked, bad control failed

content_attachment_points: decision attaches at lane t=.43 and gets highest contrast
release gate attaches at lane t=.89

module_geometry_blueprints.primary_structure_is: one oversized product surface fused with source, code, review, and proof signals

native_ppt_shape_plan.path_hint: M 34 17 L 96 8 L 90 76 L 25 86 Z; crop may extend beyond canvas on one edge
native_ppt_shape_plan.semantic_role: generated editable PPT deck or launch result
native_ppt_shape_plan.why_not_card: overscale crop, tilt, and fused perimeter details make it a revealed object rather than a container

native_ppt_shape_plan.path_hint: two curved arcs behind hero_product_surface from x=9 y=67 to x=82 y=20
native_ppt_shape_plan.semantic_role: source memory and design controls supporting the object

native_ppt_shape_plan.placement_hint: code strip along lower-left surface edge; review marker on upper-right edge; proof tags on rear rail
native_ppt_shape_plan.semantic_role: code renderer, editability, review boundary, QA proof

content_attachment_points: headline occupies open space created by the crop, not a separate card
proof labels attach to perimeter signals
support evidence stays behind or below hero_product_surface

module_geometry_blueprints.primary_structure_is_not: a checklist, table, or row of status cards
module_geometry_blueprints.primary_structure_is: a forked directional route with a visible gate and blocked public branch

native_ppt_shape_plan.semantic_role: story-ready to generated-proof to review path
native_ppt_shape_plan.why_not_card: the route is the organizing structure and content is placed on path events

native_ppt_shape_plan.bounds_hint: center x=61 y=45, angled -12deg, intersects decision_route
native_ppt_shape_plan.semantic_role: human approval and source-boundary review before release

native_ppt_shape_plan.path_hint: fork begins x=61 y=45; blocked branch curves to x=89 y=62 behind a restrained barrier

native_ppt_shape_plan.bounds_hint: x=78 y=18 w=17 h=14, attached to main route end

content_attachment_points: story ready attaches at route start

module_selection_rules.condition: The page proof object is the generated deck, editable PPT preview, or product result.
The page proof object is a source-to-memory transformation or operating stage.
The page needs to compare prompt-only failure with Vulca mechanism.
The page must expose proof states while preserving public readability.
The page needs to show generated proof, human review, blocked public release, and next run.
